title: dryandra sessilis
intro: Dryandra sessilis, also known as the Woolly Dryandra, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It belongs to the Proteaceae family and is characterized by its woolly leaves and white, pink, or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Dryandra sessilis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, with some herbalists also using it in topical creams and salves.
